summary: Israeli forces have moved within a few kilometres of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon awaiting political orders. The United States has announced cancellation of planned strikes on Iran while simultaneously conducting a second night of airstrikes on Iranian positions. Iranian officials have issued warnings of a severe response to any further U.S. attacks and have targeted Kurdish opposition sites in Erbil province.
key_points: Israeli army units have advanced to within several kilometres of the city of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon and are awaiting a political decision on occupying the city (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73440).
U.S. President Donald Trump announced the cancellation of scheduled strikes against Iran for the evening, citing approved negotiations with Iranian leadership (Source: @lelotsenzura/92885).
The United States carried out a second consecutive night of airstrikes on Iranian positions, with Trump warning of additional attacks if an agreement is not reached (Source: @mamlekate/103677).
Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned that any future U.S. attack on Iran would provoke a response more severe than any previous engagement (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
IRGC drones targeted Kurdish opposition sites in the Qoshtepa district of Erbil province, Iraq (Source: @naya_foriraq/78418).
U.S. Central Command stated that Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz and that U.S. forces are on heightened alert to defend against Iranian aggression (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/369061).
The U.S. Navy engaged three vessels in the Persian Gulf, two of which were OFAC‑sanctioned, according to India’s Foreign Ministry (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3785).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli forces have moved within a few kilometres of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon awaiting political orders. The United States has both cancelled planned strikes on Iran and conducted a second night of airstrikes against Iranian positions. Iran has issued stern warnings of a harsher response to any further U.S. attacks and has carried out drone strikes on Kurdish opposition sites in Erbil.

points: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned that any future United States attack on Iran would elicit a response more severe than any prior engagement, emphasizing regional destabilisation and the risk of a broader conflict (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
U.S. Central Command asserted that Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz and that U.S. forces are on heightened alert to defend against Iranian aggression (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/369061).
IRGC drones targeted Kurdish opposition locations in the Qoshtepa district of Erbil province, Iraq, indicating cross‑border operational reach (Source: @naya_foriraq/78418).
Iranian state media denied reports of explosions on Kish and Qeshm islands, attributing sounds to naval engagements in the Gulf (Source: @pkpoi/142666).
Israeli army units have advanced to within several kilometres of the city of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon and are awaiting a political decision on occupying the city (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73440).

The Israeli Defence Forces have established a new Air Force technician unit for ultra‑Orthodox soldiers at Tel Nof Airbase, training 20 personnel to service F‑15 fighter jets (Source: @manniefabian/58543).
Ultra‑Orthodox protesters blocked a highway in occupied Jerusalem to oppose Israeli military conscription policies (Source: @presstv/193685).
A 21‑year‑old was injured by a vehicle during a protest on Highway 1 near the Ganot interchange, receiving medical aid from MDA (Source: @N12chat/212325).
Israeli forces have moved to within a few kilometres of Nabatieh city in southern Lebanon, awaiting political leadership decisions on its capture (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73440).
Heavy military equipment transfers by the Lebanese Army to the border with Israel were reported, with speculation of an imminent ground conflict (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73443).
Early warning alerts were issued for missile launches near the Confrontation Line, affecting the city of Tzofar (Source: @tzevaadom_en/68400).
The Lebanese early‑warning system reported the end of a missile incident at the Confrontation Line shortly after (Source: @tzevaadom_en/68403).
Syrian President Bashar al‑Assad reportedly received an invitation from the United States for a visit on 14 June, according to a French‑cited diplomatic source (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73447).
IRGC drones targeted Kurdish opposition sites in Qoshtepa district, Erbil province, Iraq (Source: @naya_foriraq/78418).
U.S. Navy attacks on Iranian bases in the Strait of Hormuz were reported, occurring during a cease‑fire period (Source: @pkpoi/142667).
U.S. Navy engaged three vessels in the Persian Gulf, two of which were listed as OFAC‑sanctioned, according to India’s Foreign Ministry (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3785).
Turkey’s Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan called for Iran and the United States to cease renewed attacks and return to negotiations (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3784).
U.S. President Trump announced the cancellation of planned strikes against Iran for the evening, following high‑level negotiations involving regional partners (Source: @lelotsenzura/92885).

key_developments: Israeli army units have advanced to within several kilometres of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon and await political orders for occupation (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73440).
U.S. President Donald Trump announced the cancellation of scheduled strikes against Iran for the evening, citing approved negotiations (Source: @lelotsenzura/92885).
The United States conducted a second consecutive night of airstrikes on Iranian positions, warning of further attacks if no agreement is reached (Source: @mamlekate/103677).
Iran’s IRGC warned that any future U.S. attack would provoke a response more severe than any previous engagement, threatening regional escalation (Source: @shin_persian/6958).
IRGC drones targeted Kurdish opposition sites in Qoshtepa district, Erbil province, Iraq, demonstrating cross‑border strike capability (Source: @naya_foriraq/78418).
U.S. Central Command stated Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz and U.S. forces are on heightened alert to defend against Iranian aggression (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/369061).
The U.S. Navy engaged three vessels in the Persian Gulf, two of which were OFAC‑sanctioned, according to India’s Foreign Ministry (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3785).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment is marked by heightened Israeli activity near the Lebanese border and aggressive U.S. air operations against Iranian targets, raising the risk of direct confrontation. Iran’s explicit warnings of a harsher response and its cross‑border drone strikes in Iraq indicate a willingness to expand the conflict zone. Regional actors, including Turkey and Gulf states, are calling for de‑escalation, but the interplay of U.S. military actions and Iranian retaliation creates a volatile security calculus.
sections.regional_response: Regional governments are publicly urging restraint, with Turkey’s foreign minister urging Iran and the United States to return to negotiations, while Gulf states monitor U.S. naval engagements in the Persian Gulf. Israel’s advance toward Nabatieh has prompted diplomatic caution from its leadership, awaiting political guidance. Iran’s denial of explosions on its islands and its threats aim to shape regional narratives and deter further attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Israeli force movements near Nabatieh should be intensified to anticipate potential incursions. U.S. airstrike patterns against Iranian positions suggest a sustained campaign that may provoke Iranian retaliation. Drone activity targeting Kurdish opposition in Iraq warrants close observation for escalation beyond Iraq’s borders. Naval activity in the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical flashpoint, requiring real‑time maritime surveillance.
